items.correct: A protein containing all nine essential amino acids in adequate amounts.
items.misconception: A protein with the most calories.
items.random_wrong: Any protein from animal sources only.
items.why: Students think 'complete' means 'high quantity' or 'animal'. It means containing all ESSENTIAL amino acids the body can't make. Most animal proteins are complete; many plants need combining (e.g., rice + beans).

items.q: What is the main function of dietary fibre?
items.correct: Aiding digestion and promoting bowel health.
items.why: Students think all carbohydrates give energy. Fibre is a carb humans can't digest, so it provides NO direct energy. It bulks stools, slows glucose absorption, and feeds gut bacteria.

items.q: What is the difference between a food allergy and an intolerance?
items.correct: An allergy involves the immune system; intolerance does not.
items.misconception: They are the same with different severity.
items.random_wrong: Allergies are temporary, intolerances are permanent.
items.why: Students treat these as severity points on one spectrum. They have different mechanisms - allergies trigger immune (IgE) responses (potentially fatal anaphylaxis); intolerances are digestive (e.g., lactase deficiency).
Correct answer: Matchsticks. “Cubes” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Correct answer: Leaves. “Hard veg” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Roughly how many mm is a brunoise dice?
items.why: Exact answer: 2. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
